3	import (
4		"context"
5		"io"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"time"

10	// Config holds agent service configuration.
11	type Config struct {
12		Profile         string              `yaml:"profile"`          // default routing intent: default, cheap, fast, smart
13		Harness         string              `yaml:"harness"`          // optional forced harness override
14		Model           string              `yaml:"model"`            // optional default model ref or exact pin
15		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models"`           // per-harness model overrides
16		ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
17		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
18		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
19		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
20		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted
21	}
22	
23	// RouteFlags holds raw CLI flag values before normalization into a RouteRequest.
24	// These come directly from parsed command-line arguments.
25	type RouteFlags struct {
26		Profile     string // --profile: default, cheap, fast, smart
27		Model       string // --model: logical ref or exact pin
28		Provider    string // --provider: explicit provider name
29		ModelRef    string // --model-ref: catalog model reference
30		Harness     string // --harness: forced harness override
31		Effort      string // --effort: low, medium, high
32		Permissions string // --permissions: safe, supervised, unrestricted
33	}
34	
35	// RunArgs holds options for a single agent invocation.
36	type RunArgs struct {
37		// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
38		// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
39		// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
40		// Nil defaults to context.Background().
41		Context       context.Context
42		Harness       string
43		Prompt        string // prompt text (or path to file)
44		PromptFile    string // explicit file path
45		PromptSource  string
46		Correlation   map[string]string
47		Model         string
48		Provider      string // explicit provider name (e.g. "vidar", "openrouter"); bypasses default provider selection
49		ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref (e.g. "code-medium"); resolved via the model catalog
50		Effort        string
51		Timeout       time.Duration // idle (inactivity) timeout; nonzero overrides Config.TimeoutMS
52		WallClock     time.Duration // absolute wall-clock cap; nonzero overrides Config.WallClockMS
53		WorkDir       string
54		Permissions   string // permission level override: safe, supervised, unrestricted
55		SessionLogDir string // per-run override for session log dir; used by execute-bead to redirect embedded-agent runtime state out of the worktree root
56	}
57	
58	// AgentRunRuntime is the SD-024 successor to RunArgs for the agent
59	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
60	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
61	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
62	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
63	// runtime intent remain.
64	//
65	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
66	type AgentRunRuntime struct {
67		Prompt                string
68		PromptFile            string
69		PromptSource          string
70		Output                io.Writer
71		Correlation           map[string]string
72		Record                bool
73		Replay                bool
74		WorkDir               string
75		EstimatedPromptTokens int
76		RequiresTools         bool
77		SessionLogDirOverride string
78		// HarnessOverride, ModelOverride, and PermissionsOverride let a caller
79		// pin one of the durable knobs from rcfg for this single invocation
80		// without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig. SD-024 step B22d-d:
81		// execute-bead pins permissions=unrestricted; the post-merge reviewer
82		// pins harness/model to the reviewer-tier selection.
83		HarnessOverride     string
84		ModelOverride       string
85		PermissionsOverride string
86	}

376	// Default configuration values.
377	const (
378		DefaultHarness = "codex"
379		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
380		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
381		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
382		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
383		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
384		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
385		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
386		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
387		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
388		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
389		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
390		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
391		DefaultLogDir      = ".ddx/agent-logs"
392	)

394	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
395	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
396	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
397	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
398	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
399		if configured == "" {
400			configured = DefaultLogDir
401		}
402		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
403			return configured
404		}
405		if projectRoot == "" {
406			return configured
407		}
408		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
409	}

58	// BenchmarkArmsToCompare converts a slice of BenchmarkArms into a
59	// CompareRuntime carrying per-arm harness/model/label data plus the
60	// caller-provided base AgentRunRuntime (Prompt, PromptFile, WorkDir, ...).
61	func BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms []BenchmarkArm, base AgentRunRuntime) CompareRuntime {
62		harnesses := make([]string, len(arms))
63		armModels := make(map[int]string, len(arms))
64		armLabels := make(map[int]string, len(arms))
65	
66		for i, arm := range arms {
67			arm.ResolveArm()
68			harnesses[i] = arm.Harness
69			if arm.Model != "" {
70				armModels[i] = arm.Model
71			}
72			armLabels[i] = arm.Label
73		}
74	
75		return CompareRuntime{
76			AgentRunRuntime: base,
77			Harnesses:       harnesses,
78			ArmModels:       armModels,
79			ArmLabels:       armLabels,
80		}
81	}

45	// RunWithConfigViaService dispatches a single agent invocation through the
46	// agent service. It takes a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
47	// AgentRunRuntime (per-invocation plumbing/intent), and is the SD-024
48	// successor to the retired RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith entry points.
49	//
50	// The virtual and script harnesses route through a DDx-owned Runner path,
51	// not the upstream service. These are not "carve-outs pending migration" —
52	// they are different products from upstream's same-named stubs. DDx's
53	// virtual is a content-addressed record/replay dictionary keyed by
54	// PromptHash; upstream's is a unit-test stub where callers stuff
55	// virtual.response into Metadata. DDx's script reads a filesystem directive
56	// file; upstream does not model this at all.
57	func RunWithConfigViaService(ctx context.Context, workDir string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
58		if ctx == nil {
59			ctx = context.Background()
60		}
61	
62		harness := rcfg.Passthrough().Harness
63	
64		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
65			sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
66			if sessionLogDir == "" {
67				sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
68			}
69			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(workDir, "")}
70			r := NewRunner(cfg)
71			r.WorkDir = workDir
72			return r.runInternal(RunArgs{
73				Context:       ctx,
74				Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
75				Prompt:        runtime.Prompt,
76				PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
77				PromptSource:  runtime.PromptSource,
78				Correlation:   runtime.Correlation,
79				Model:         rcfg.Model(),
80				Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
81				ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
82				Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
83				Timeout:       rcfg.Timeout(),
84				WallClock:     rcfg.WallClock(),
85				WorkDir:       runtime.WorkDir,
86				Permissions:   rcfg.Permissions(),
87				SessionLogDir: sessionLogDir,
88			})
89		}
90	
91		factory := serviceRunFactory
92		if factory == nil {
93			factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
94		}
95		svc, err := factory(workDir)
96		if err != nil {

55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}

105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d

83	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
84	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
85	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
86	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
87	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
88	// SuccessStatus.
89	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
90		out := EscalationSummary{
91			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
92			WinningTier:    winningTier,
93		}
94		if out.WinningTier == "" {
95			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
96		}
97		for _, a := range attempts {
98			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
99			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
100				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
101			}
102		}
103		return out
104	}

376	// Default configuration values.
377	const (
378		DefaultHarness = "codex"
379		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
380		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
381		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
382		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
383		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
384		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
385		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
386		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
387		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
388		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
389		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
390		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
391		DefaultLogDir      = ".ddx/agent-logs"
392	)
393	
394	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
395	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
396	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
397	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
398	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
399		if configured == "" {
400			configured = DefaultLogDir
401		}
402		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
403			return configured
404		}
405		if projectRoot == "" {
406			return configured
407		}
408		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
409	}

7	// harnessToSurface maps harness names to their catalog surface identifier.

14		"opencode": "claude",
15	}

17	// ResolveModelTier returns the concrete model for a given harness and tier
18	// by looking up the tier profile in BuiltinCatalog for the harness's surface.
19	func ResolveModelTier(harness string, tier escalation.ModelTier) string {
20		surface, ok := harnessToSurface[harness]
21		if !ok {
22			return ""
23		}
24		model, _ := BuiltinCatalog.Resolve(string(tier), surface)
25		return model
26	}
27	
28	// BenchmarkArm defines one arm in a benchmark run.
29	type BenchmarkArm struct {
30		Label   string               `json:"label"`
31		Harness string               `json:"harness"`
32		Tier    escalation.ModelTier `json:"tier"`
33		Model   string               `json:"model,omitempty"` // explicit override; empty = resolve from tier
34	}
35	
36	// DefaultBenchmarkArms returns the standard set of arms for a full comparison.
37	func DefaultBenchmarkArms() []BenchmarkArm {
38		return []BenchmarkArm{
39			{Label: "agent-smart", Harness: "agent", Tier: escalation.TierSmart},
40			{Label: "agent-standard", Harness: "agent", Tier: escalation.TierStandard},
41			{Label: "agent-cheap", Harness: "agent", Tier: escalation.TierCheap},
42			{Label: "codex-smart", Harness: "codex", Tier: escalation.TierSmart},
43			{Label: "codex-standard", Harness: "codex", Tier: escalation.TierStandard},
44			{Label: "codex-cheap", Harness: "codex", Tier: escalation.TierCheap},
45			{Label: "claude-smart", Harness: "claude", Tier: escalation.TierSmart},
46			{Label: "claude-standard", Harness: "claude", Tier: escalation.TierStandard},
47			{Label: "claude-cheap", Harness: "claude", Tier: escalation.TierCheap},
48		}
49	}
50	
51	// ResolveArm fills in the model from the tier if not explicitly set.
52	func (a *BenchmarkArm) ResolveArm() {
53		if a.Model == "" {
54			a.Model = ResolveModelTier(a.Harness, a.Tier)
55		}
56	}
57	
58	// BenchmarkArmsToCompare converts a slice of BenchmarkArms into a
59	// CompareRuntime carrying per-arm harness/model/label data plus the
60	// caller-provided base AgentRunRuntime (Prompt, PromptFile, WorkDir, ...).
61	func BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms []BenchmarkArm, base AgentRunRuntime) CompareRuntime {
62		harnesses := make([]string, len(arms))
63		armModels := make(map[int]string, len(arms))
64		armLabels := make(map[int]string, len(arms))
65	
66		for i, arm := range arms {
67			arm.ResolveArm()
68			harnesses[i] = arm.Harness
69			if arm.Model != "" {
70				armModels[i] = arm.Model
71			}
72			armLabels[i] = arm.Label
73		}
74	
75		return CompareRuntime{
76			AgentRunRuntime: base,
77			Harnesses:       harnesses,
78			ArmModels:       armModels,
79			ArmLabels:       armLabels,
80		}
81	}
